findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/HilbertBasisMap.lean:85-86`, `:92-93`, and `:99-100` prove the new lemmas by `simpa only [HilbertBasis.mapₗᵢ_apply] using (b.mapₗᵢ e).…`; Mathlib already provides `HilbertBasis.hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `tsum_inner_mul_inner` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/InnerProductSpace/l2Space.lean:457`, `:463`, and `:467`.
findings.fix: Remove these three declarations and use `(b.mapₗᵢ e).hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `(b.mapₗᵢ e).summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `(b.mapₗᵢ e).tsum_inner_mul_inner` with `simp [HilbertBasis.mapₗᵢ_apply]` at call sites.
findings.issue: `mapₗᵢ_hasSum_inner_mul_inner`, `mapₗᵢ_summable_inner_mul_inner`, and `mapₗᵢ_tsum_inner_mul_inner` duplicate the existing Parseval API specialized to `b.mapₗᵢ e` and rewritten by `mapₗᵢ_apply`.

summary: The added transported Parseval API is only a rewritten specialization of existing HilbertBasis Parseval lemmas, so it creates parallel API instead of reusing the generic form.
